name: Jared Brewer, Ph.D.
title: Data Scientist
tagline: Experienced immunologist applying bioinformatics approaches to oncology
experience: 8+ years of experience studying microbiology and immunology, 5+ years in bioinformatics and computational biology
expertise: Spatial biology, image analysis, RNA-seq, single-cell technologies, immunoprofiling, database mining
bio: Jared comes from an experimental biology background studying the intersection between bacterial pathogenesis, innate immune signaling, and cell biological  responses in tuberculosis infection using multimodal approaches during his Ph.D. in Molecular Genetics and Microbiology at Duke University. At Vindhya, he has applied the computational skills developed during these studies to immuno-oncology, helping to identify biomarkers both from RNA-seq and scRNA-seq approaches as well as spatial profiling, including spatial proteomics and developing neural networks to study cell-cell interactions.

name: Eric Davis, Ph.D.
title: Data Engineer
expertise: Genomics, 3D chromatin structure, Software development, Pipeline engineering, Interactive data visualization, Reproducible research
bio: Eric holds a Ph.D. in Bioinformatics and Computational Biology from UNC-Chapel Hill where he studied how DNA loops regulate gene transcription in human development and disease. In addition to his Bioinformatics knowledge, Eric is a full-stack software developer. He has built Bioconductor packages, R Shiny web applications, interactive data visualization dashboards, and consulted as a web developer. Eric is passionate about reproducible research, using technology like Docker/Singularity along with Snakemake/Nextflow to build and execute bioinformatic pipelines on HPC systems and in the cloud. At Vindhya, Eric has helped clients build high-quality software packages and bolster their reproducible research infrastructure.

name: Chinmaya Joisa, Ph.D.
title: Data Scientist
tagline: Computational biologist applying bioinformatics and predictive modeling to oncology
experience: 7 years of bench and computational experience in pharmacology and biomedical engineering, specialising in applications to systems biology
expertise: Machine Learning in Biology, Bioinformatic Pipeline development, Proteomics, Genomics
bio: Chinmaya Joisa is a computational biologist coming from a background of kinase biology and cancer cell signalling, where he developed interpretable AI methods to build predictive models of cancer response to kinase inhibitors during his PhD in Biomedical Engineering at UNC-Chapel Hill. At Vindhya he is committed to utilize his expertise at the intersection of computational and experimental biology to meaningfully impact drug development and therapeutics.

name: Alfred Simkin, Ph.D.
title: Data Engineer
tagline: Bioinformatics Scientist, Educator, and Software Engineer
experience: 15 years of bioinformatics and teaching experience in a mixture of education, academic bioinformatics cores, and industry
expertise: training and user support, workflow development, simulation, Python, cloud computing, efficient algorithm design, variant calling, gene expression analysis, customizable genome browsers
bio: After obtaining his PhD at Umass medical school in 2014 in the evolution of noncoding RNA networks, Alfred completed postdoctoral training at Cornell in 2016 simulating neutrally evolving 3'UTRs. Alfred then took a tenure track teaching position at Elon University where he taught genetics until 2021. This was followed by work in a bioinformatics core as a research professor at Umass medical school, then industry work at IDEXX as a staff scientist, and then a research professor position at Brown University developing software and training materials to visualize the spread of malaria drug resistance mutations, where he still holds a part-time position. Alfred loves teaching bioinformatics and programming, automating and scaling pipelines, debugging, and learning new skills.

name: Joseph Bundy, Ph.D.
title: Data Scientist
tagline: Experienced computational biologist and R programmer
experience: 10+ years of experience analyzing transcriptomics data with R, 6+ years in machine learning and analysis of chemical bioactivity data
expertise: RNA-seq, Proteomics, Machine learning, High-throughput screening, Molecular biology, Neuroscience
bio: Joe holds a Ph.D. in Biomedical Sciences from Florida State University and has a background in bioinformatics and computational toxicology.  Joe has extensive experience analyzing 'omics' data from a variety of model systems including mouse, zebrafish, and human cancer cell lines. Before joining Vindhya, Joe was part of the Center for Computational Toxicology and Exposure at the U.S. EPA where he developed expertise in predicting chemical mechanism of action from High Throughput Transcriptomics datasets.
